## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so now I want to talk about the circle of awesome because, well, it's just plain awesome. So this is the deal is, I don't want you to think about just one launch. Yeah, we got to get that first launch under your belt, but I want you to think in terms of sequences of launches. And this is how John Gallagher goes from food stamps to having a million dollar business. This is Susan Garrett, complete newbie, and first does that first launch and then does hundreds of thousands of dollars. This is how I've built my business. So there's a few different ways this can go, but I'm going to show you all of them, or most of them at least. You can get creative if you want. But basically what you always want to do is, there's this thing called the JV launch down here. So JV launches, you can make a lot of money. I mean, because you're tapping into other people's lists, JV means joint venture, so you can use affiliates or joint venture partners. And they mail their list about your launch. And I mean, what if you had a 10,000 person list, but you could find five joint venture partners and each of those people had a 20,000 person list, and all of a sudden 100,000 people, between the five of them, 100,000 people would be emailed instead of 10,000 people on your list so that it gives you a universe that's literally 10 times bigger than your universe, which is five partners. Something like that is not unusual. You can just like, I mean, the biggest launch I ever did before my first JV launch, the biggest launch I ever did was an internal launch of 100,000, $110,000. My first JV launch was over $600,000, so it was a factor of 6X. My next joint venture launch after that was almost $4 million. So it's a factor of 40X over my internal launches. So joint venture launches can be really, really great. The thing is, is you don't want your first launch to be a joint venture launch because you want to test your, you want to test your launch, you want to test your pre-launch content, you want to test your sideways sales ladder to make sure it converts before you ask partners to mail for you. Because if those partners mail for you, you want to make a lot of money for them so they want to mail for you in the future. Does it make sense? Okay, so that means that you don't test out your launch on a JV launch. What you do is you do an internal launch first. So you do an internal launch and you figure out your offer, you figure out your pre-launch content, you figure out the whole sequence, you figure out your open cart sequence, you figure out how to take care of people after they buy from you. So with the Circle of Awesome, basically what you do is you start off and you test the process. So maybe you have a list of a thousand people. Let's just say you have a thousand people on your list. And you do this internal launch and you do, I don't know, let's say we do $20,000 on that

internal launch. Okay, then what you do is you go to your joint venture partners and you say, hey, I've got this product. ## [09:02 - 12:04]

You're going through your second launch here. You've built up 15,000 people. And now what happens is you get more ideas because the market just to keep on talking to you. ## [12:04 - 12:40]

Get that initial tiny little list that's built through that seed launch process. And then go to an internal launch from there. And then take it to a JV launch. So there's a lot of different ways, but that's the way these three can tie together. And that's why in this course, I'm teaching that seed launch first. And then we're going to go through the internal launch and the JV launch. So you can totally get this circle of awesome and put it to use in your business. Because it is 100% awesome.
